title: Mailbox Message Send and Storage
interaction_flow: Caller → MailboxStore: send({ type, payload, channel, priority, refId, expiresAt })
MailboxStore → safeParse(payload): parse string payload to JSON if needed

MailboxStore: constructs msg { id, channel (default 'evomap-hub'), direction:'outbound', type, status:'pending', payload, priority (default 'normal'), ref_id, created_at:Date.now(), synced_at:null, expires_at, retry_count:0, error:null }
MailboxStore → Caller: return { message_id: id, status: 'pending' }
Caller → MailboxStore: writeInbound({ id?, type, payload, channel, priority, refId, expiresAt })
MailboxStore → generateUUIDv7(): called only if id not provided
MailboxStore → safeParse(payload): parse string payload to JSON if needed
MailboxStore: constructs msg { id:msgId, channel, direction:'inbound', type, status:'pending', payload, priority, ref_id, created_at, synced_at:null, expires_at, retry_count:0, error:null }
MailboxStore: iterates messages → calls writeInbound(m) for each
_appendUpdate → _messages.get(id) then Object.assign(existing, fields): patch in-memory record
MailboxStore → readLines(messagesFile): fs.readFileSync + split('\n') + JSON.parse each line
MailboxStore: for each row — if _op='update': apply fields to existing; else _messages.set(row.id, row)
MailboxStore: filter out terminal statuses {synced, delivered, failed, rejected}
MailboxStore: populate _outbound[] or _inbound[] based on msg.direction
key_relationships: MailboxStore owns _outbound[] and _inbound[] — ordered ID arrays for pending messages
MailboxStore owns _state{} — key-value store for cursors and metadata
MailboxStore persists messages to messages.jsonl (append-only JSONL)
appendLine wraps fs.appendFileSync for JSONL rows
readLines wraps fs.readFileSync + line-by-line JSON.parse (skips corrupt lines)
writeInboundBatch() delegates to writeInbound() per message
_appendUpdate() writes { _op:'update', id, fields } record to JSONL + patches in-memory Map
_rebuildIndex() replays full JSONL log to reconstruct in-memory state
important_notes: JSONL is append-only; updates are delta records ({ _op:'update' }), not rewrites
_rebuildIndex() is called on construction and after compact() to replay the JSONL log
compact() rewrites messages.jsonl with only current message states (no update deltas), reducing file size
UUIDv7 IDs are time-ordered (48-bit unix_ts_ms prefix), enabling chronological sorting
safeParse() handles both string (JSON-parses) and object payloads transparently
state.json stores cursors (prefixed 'cursor:<key>') and arbitrary key-value state
Migration system: MIGRATIONS registry keyed by target schema version, runs on _loadState() if version mismatch
No async I/O — all file operations are synchronous (appendFileSync, readFileSync, writeFileSync)
